compiled_state.title: Back-compat alias window for legacy .rebar/config.conf keys
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Old flat .rebar/config.conf must read IDENTICALLY for >=1 release; alias legacy keys (verify.require_verdict_for_close -> require_signature_for_close); unknown keys WARN (not fail) during deprecation, hard-error only after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Legacy flat config still parsed; legacy key aliases honored
- [ ] Unknown-key WARN during deprecation window (configurable cutover)
- [ ] Tests for legacy-config parity + alias + warn-not-fail
Blocked by the route-reads task.

comments.body: NOTE (from 252e review): legacy-key alias resolution (verify.require_verdict_for_close -> require_signature_for_close) currently lives inside the per-section parse. For correct layering, resolve legacy aliases at the SPARSE-MERGE stage (so a legacy key in a lower layer and the new key in a higher layer compare/precede correctly), not only at final parse.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
DONE (config-loop, commit 3656764a). Back-compat for the legacy flat .rebar/config.conf. AC1 (legacy still parsed + aliases honored): legacy dotted-key parsing (_read_legacy_conf) and the verify.require_verdict_for_close->require_signature_for_close alias (_ALIASES, resolved per-layer in coerce_sparse) were already in place from 43a0/252e; LOCKED IN with parity tests — the SAME settings via legacy conf vs rebar.toml resolve to an IDENTICAL typed Config; comment/quoted-value/spaces-around-=/keyless-line handling verified. Confirmed the 252e review property: aliases resolve per-layer to canonical BEFORE merge, so a legacy key in a lower layer + canonical in a higher layer compares by canonical name (higher wins) — test added. AC2 (unknown-key WARN during window, hard-error after, CONFIGURABLE cutover): added a  flag threaded through coerce_sparse / Config.from_mapping / _ordered_layers; default WARN (deprecation window, unchanged) -> REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error makes unknown sections/keys raise ConfigError. Strict is ORTHOGONAL to value validation (invalid values always fail-closed regardless) and to aliasing (a recognized legacy key still WARNs + is accepted under strict, never errors). Added the env var to the load_config cache signature so toggling the policy invalidates cached resolutions. Also made # rebar resolved configuration
# project config: (none found)
# user config:    /Users/joeoakhart/.config/rebar/config.toml (none)
# precedence:     default < user < project < env < cli

verify.require_signature_for_close = false  [default]
ticket.display_mode                = auto   [default]
compact.threshold                  = 10     [default]
sync.push                          = always [default]
sync.pull                          = on     [default]
mcp.readonly                       = false  [default]
mcp.allow_llm                      = false  [default]
mcp.allow_jira_sync                = false  [default]
reconciler.jira_cli_timeout        = 0      [default]
reconciler.lock_max_retries        = 5      [default]
reconciler.deletion_probe_limit    = 20     [default]
reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe  = false  [default]
jira.url                           = ""     [default]
jira.user                          = ""     [default]
jira.project                       = ""     [default]
scratch.base_dir                   = ""     [default] report ConfigError as a clean stderr message + exit 1 (no traceback). AC3 (tests): 12 back-compat tests + 1 CLI-error test covering all of the above. Subagent review: NO critical/major; verify-gate fail-closed preserved, alias-before-unknown-check confirmed, cache key sufficient; 1 minor (strict fails fast on first unknown SECTION) accepted as load-time behavior. Full offline suite green: 1912 passed, 9 skipped.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

file_impact.reason: strict unknown-key cutover threaded through coercion + _strict_unknown_keys + cache sig
clean ConfigError handling in rebar config
